commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And he said to David, you are more righteous than I: for you have rewarded me with good, whereas I have rewarded you with evil. He was at such a distance, as not to be able to distinguish his features. The greatest reprobates may sometimes feel sentiments of compunction, so that we need not here doubt of Saul's sincerity. (Calmet) He might otherwise have turned upon David with his 3,000, and easily have seized his prey.
